description:
You are a mobile build engineer who has just been asked to perform a small maintenance task on your CI/CD scripts.  
Everything you need is already on-disk in:

/home/user/projects/pipeline/

Inside that directory you will find two configuration files that the build system consumes:

1. /home/user/projects/pipeline/pipeline.yaml  
2. /home/user/projects/pipeline/config.toml  

Goal  
Edit those two files so that the pipeline gains an integration-test stage and the release section reflects a production rollout.  
After you make the edits, create a short plain-text log that lists exactly what you changed.

Detailed requirements (what the automated checker will verify)

A. /home/user/projects/pipeline/pipeline.yaml – final state  
   1. The value of the top-level key version must be the integer 2 (it is currently 1).  
   2. The list under the key stages must become:  
      - build  
      - test  
      - integration  
      (i.e., append the integration stage in that precise order).  
   3. Under jobs create a new job definition with the exact YAML structure shown below (indentation is 2 spaces per level):  
      integration_test:  
        stage: integration  
        script:  
          - echo "Running integration tests"  
      The existing jobs (build_app and run_tests) must remain unchanged and in place.

B. /home/user/projects/pipeline/config.toml – final state  
   1. In the [build] table add the key/value:  
      parallel_jobs = 4  
      (leave existing keys untouched).  
   2. In the [release] table:  
      • Increment version_code by 1 (i.e., if it is 42 it must become 43).  
      • Change track so that it reads:  
        track = "production"  
   3. Append a brand-new table to the end of the file with exactly this content:  
      [notifications]  
      email = "ci-alerts@example.com"  
      slack = "#ci-alerts"

C. /home/user/projects/pipeline/update.log – creation & format  
   1. Create the file /home/user/projects/pipeline/update.log (it does not exist yet).  
   2. It must contain exactly four lines, no extra whitespace, each describing one change, in this precise order:

pipeline.yaml - version updated to 2  
pipeline.yaml - stage 'integration' and job 'integration_test' added  
config.toml - version_code updated to 43  
config.toml - production track, parallel_jobs, notifications added  

No timestamps or additional commentary are allowed; the automated checker performs a strict line-by-line comparison.

Practical notes  
• You do NOT need root or sudo for any part of this task.  
• Stay entirely inside /home/user/projects/pipeline/.  
• Use standard command-line tools available in a minimal Linux environment (e.g., sed, echo, printf, printf with heredoc, etc.).  
• Do not rename or relocate any files.  
• Make sure the file permissions allow the current user to read and write the three files (default umask is fine).  

When you are done, the pipeline should be ready for a production deployment and the update.log file will give reviewers an at-a-glance overview of your edits.
truth:
Initial files provided to the agent before the task starts:

1. /home/user/projects/pipeline/pipeline.yaml  
------------------------------------------------
version: 1
stages:
  - build
  - test
jobs:
  build_app:
    stage: build
    script:
      - echo "Building Android app"
  run_tests:
    stage: test
    script:
      - echo "Running unit tests"

2. /home/user/projects/pipeline/config.toml  
-------------------------------------------
[build]
android_sdk = "/opt/android/sdk"
ios_sdk     = "/Applications/Xcode.app"

[release]
version_code = 42
track        = "beta"

Directory layout prior to execution:
/home/user/projects/pipeline/               (0755)
/home/user/projects/                        (0755)
/home/user/                                 (0755)

No other files are present.

Expected final state after successful execution:

1. /home/user/projects/pipeline/pipeline.yaml  
------------------------------------------------
version: 2
stages:
  - build
  - test
  - integration
jobs:
  build_app:
    stage: build
    script:
      - echo "Building Android app"
  run_tests:
    stage: test
    script:
      - echo "Running unit tests"
  integration_test:
    stage: integration
    script:
      - echo "Running integration tests"

2. /home/user/projects/pipeline/config.toml  
-------------------------------------------
[build]
android_sdk = "/opt/android/sdk"
ios_sdk     = "/Applications/Xcode.app"
parallel_jobs = 4

[release]
version_code = 43
track        = "production"

[notifications]
email = "ci-alerts@example.com"
slack = "#ci-alerts"

3. /home/user/projects/pipeline/update.log  
-------------------------------------------
pipeline.yaml - version updated to 2
pipeline.yaml - stage 'integration' and job 'integration_test' added
config.toml - version_code updated to 43
config.toml - production track, parallel_jobs, notifications added

All three files must be readable and writable by the current user (0644 or 0664 is acceptable).  
There must be no other modifications in the directory.
